title: Flood Switch Activated
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base of the dishwasher, activating the flood protection switch.
fullDescription: Fault F1 indicates the anti-flood switch in the base of the dishwasher has detected water where it should not be. This safety feature shuts down the machine and activates the drain pump to remove excess water. Common causes include a leaking hose connection, cracked sump, worn door seal, or a faulty inlet valve that drips.

causes.cause: Leaking internal hose connection or clamp
Worn or damaged door gasket allowing water to escape
Cracked sump or wash pump housing
Faulty flood switch giving false alarm

symptoms: F1 fault code flashing on display
Machine will not start a wash cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the water supply tap to the dishwasher. Unplug the machine from the power outlet. Place towels around the base to absorb any water.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the base tray
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the dishwasher back or pull it out from the cabinet. Check the base tray for water. Use a sponge or towels to remove the water. Look for the source of the leak.

fixSteps.title: Check hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all internal hose connections, particularly at the sump, wash pump, and drain pump. Tighten any loose clamps. Replace any hoses that are cracked or perished.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door gasket around the entire perimeter. Look for tears, deformation, or buildup preventing a proper seal. Clean the seal and mating surfaces. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Dry the flood switch
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flood switch (usually a polystyrene float in the base tray). Ensure the float moves freely and is not stuck in the raised position. Dry the base tray thoroughly before restoring power.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source is a cracked sump or wash pump housing, professional replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F1 fault flood switch fix

faq.q: What does F1 mean on a Fisher & Paykel dishwasher?
faq.a: F1 means water has been detected in the base of the dishwasher. The flood protection system has activated to prevent damage.

faq.q: Why does the drain pump keep running?
faq.a: When the flood switch activates, the drain pump runs continuously to remove water from the base tray. This is a safety feature.

faq.q: Can I reset F1 without fixing the leak?
faq.a: You can temporarily clear it by drying the base tray, but F1 will return if the underlying leak is not repaired.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher did not fill with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Fault F2 indicates the dishwasher attempted to fill with water but the required level was not reached within the timeout period. This is commonly caused by a closed water supply tap, kinked inlet hose, blocked filter screen, or a faulty inlet valve.
symptoms: Machine beeps and stops at beginning of cycle
Inlet valve may buzz but no water flows
Dishes remain dry after cycle attempt

fixSteps.title: Check water supply tap
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water supply tap (usually under the kitchen sink) and ensure it is fully open. Turn it off and on to confirm water flows.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the water inlet hose for kinks or sharp bends. Straighten the hose and ensure it has a clear path without being pinched behind the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter screen
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the inlet hose from the dishwasher. Remove the small mesh filter screen and clean it under running water. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, access the inlet valve (usually at the bottom). Test the solenoid with a multimeter for continuity. Replace the valve if it tests open circuit.

whenToCallPro: If the tap is open, hose is clear, and valve tests faulty, you can replace it yourself or call a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F2 fault water fill timeout fix

faq.q: What does F2 mean on my dishwasher?
faq.a: F2 means the dishwasher could not fill with water. Check the water supply tap first.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause F2?
faq.a: Yes. If household water pressure is very low, the dishwasher may not fill within the allowed time.

faq.q: How do I reset the F2 fault?
faq.a: Fix the water supply issue, then turn the dishwasher off and on. The fault will clear if water flows normally.

title: Drain Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher is unable to drain water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Fault F3 indicates the drain system has failed to remove water from the dishwasher within the timeout period. Causes include a blocked drain filter, clogged drain hose, faulty drain pump, or a blocked sink drain connection.

causes.cause: Blocked drain filter or food debris in sump
Blocked sink drain or garbage disposal connection

symptoms: Water standing in the bottom of the dishwasher
Drain pump hums but water does not drain

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom rack. Lift out the drain filter assembly from the bottom of the dishwasher. Rinse it thoroughly under running water, removing all food debris and grease.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher forward slightly to access the drain hose. Check for kinks or clogs. Disconnect and flush the hose with water if necessary.

fixSteps.title: Clear the sink connection
fixSteps.instruction: If the drain hose connects to a garbage disposal, ensure the knockout plug was removed during installation. If connected to the sink drain, check the air gap or high loop for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump underneath the dishwasher. Check for debris in the pump housing. Test the pump motor with a multimeter — it should show continuity. Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning filters and hoses does not work and the drain pump is dead, professional replacement is straightforward.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F3 fault drain failure fix

faq.q: What does F3 mean?
faq.a: F3 means the dishwasher cannot drain water. Usually caused by a blocked filter or drain hose.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter after every few cycles or at least weekly to prevent blockages.

faq.q: Can a garbage disposal cause F3?
faq.a: Yes. If the knockout plug was not removed during installation, or the disposal is clogged, water cannot drain.

title: Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (NTC) is reading out of range or has failed.
fullDescription: Fault F4 indicates the NTC thermistor is providing readings outside the acceptable range. Without accurate temperature data, the dishwasher cannot properly heat water for washing and sanitizing.

causes.cause: Faulty NTC thermistor (open or short circuit)

symptoms: Water not heated during wash cycle
Dishes come out cold and not clean

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and unplug it for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and try a cycle. A temporary sensor glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Access and test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower access panel. Locate the NTC thermistor (usually near the heating element or sump). Disconnect and test with a multimeter — should read 10-20 kOhms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the sensor to the control board. Look for damaged insulation, loose connectors, or water damage. Reseat all connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests faulty, remove it from its housing and install the replacement. Reconnect the wiring and run a test cycle to confirm proper heating.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor replacement does not fix the issue, the control board may need inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F4 temperature sensor fix

faq.q: What does F4 mean?
faq.a: F4 means the water temperature sensor has failed. The dishwasher cannot regulate water heating.

faq.q: Can I still run the dishwasher with F4?
faq.a: The dishwasher may run but will not heat water properly, resulting in poor cleaning and no sanitization.

faq.q: Is the temperature sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, the NTC sensor is one of the cheaper parts, typically $10-$30.

title: Rotor Position Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The wash motor rotor position sensor has failed or lost signal.
fullDescription: Fault F5 indicates the control board cannot detect the wash motor position. The rotor sensor (Hall effect sensor) provides feedback on motor speed and position. Without this signal, the dishwasher cannot properly control wash spray pressure and direction.

causes.cause: Faulty Hall effect sensor on wash motor
Loose connector between sensor and control board

symptoms: Poor wash performance — low pressure spraying
Machine runs but no water circulation heard

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes and restore power. Test with a cycle. Temporary signal interruptions may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the wash motor underneath the dishwasher. Check that the sensor connector is firmly attached. Reseat all connections.

fixSteps.title: Test the rotor sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. Check for the specified resistance value in the service manual. Replace if readings are abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace wash motor assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is integrated into the motor and cannot be replaced separately, the entire wash motor assembly may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor is integrated into the motor, professional replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F5 rotor sensor fix

faq.q: What does F5 mean?
faq.a: F5 means the wash motor position sensor has failed. The dishwasher cannot properly control the wash cycle.

faq.q: Can I replace just the sensor?
faq.a: On some models the sensor is separate; on others it is integrated into the motor and the whole assembly must be replaced.

faq.q: Is F5 a common fault?
faq.a: F5 is less common than fill or drain faults but can occur on older units or after power surges.

title: Water Inlet Valve Fault
shortDescription: The water inlet valve is not responding to control signals.
fullDescription: Fault F6 indicates the control board attempted to activate the water inlet valve but did not get the expected response. The valve solenoid may be faulty, or the control board relay may have failed.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded wiring at valve connector
Power supply issue to valve circuit

symptoms: No water entering dishwasher despite supply being on
Click heard from valve area but no water flow
Previously worked fine then suddenly stopped filling

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the water supply tap is on and there is adequate pressure. This rules out supply issues vs valve issues.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the inlet valve. Disconnect the wiring and test the solenoid coil with a multimeter. It should read 500-2000 ohms. Infinite resistance means the coil is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring to valve
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring connector at the valve for corrosion or loose pins. Follow the wiring back to the control board and check for damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off the water supply. Disconnect the water line and electrical connector. Remove mounting screws and install the new valve.

whenToCallPro: If the valve tests good but the fault persists, the control board relay may need professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F6 inlet valve fix

faq.q: What does F6 mean?
faq.a: F6 means the water inlet valve is not responding. The solenoid may be burned out.

faq.q: How is F6 different from F2?
faq.a: F2 is a fill timeout (water does not reach the level), while F6 specifically identifies a valve electrical fault.

faq.q: Can I clean the inlet valve instead of replacing it?
faq.a: You can clean the filter screen, but if the solenoid coil has failed, the valve must be replaced.

title: Heating Element Fault
shortDescription: The wash heating element has failed or is not heating water to the target temperature.
fullDescription: Fault F7 indicates the heating element is not raising water temperature as expected. The control board monitors temperature rise over time and triggers this fault when heating performance is insufficient. The element may be burned out, have a ground fault, or a relay may have failed.

causes.cause: Heater relay failure on control board
Wiring fault between element and control board

symptoms: Dishes come out greasy or not clean
Cycle completes but dishes are cold

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the heating element beneath the tub. Disconnect the wiring and test with a multimeter. A working element reads 15-30 ohms. Infinite resistance means it is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Check for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Test between each heater terminal and the metal chassis. Any continuity reading indicates a ground fault — the element must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring from the element terminals to the control board for burn marks, loose connections, or damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the retaining bracket and disconnect wiring. Carefully remove the old element from underneath the tub. Install the new element with a fresh gasket and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the element tests fine, the heater relay on the control board likely needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F7 heating element fix

faq.q: What does F7 mean?
faq.a: F7 means the heating element is not heating water to the required temperature.

faq.q: Can a ground fault be dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A ground fault in the heating element can cause electric shock and should be repaired immediately.

faq.q: Will the dishwasher still run with F7?
faq.a: Some models will run a cold wash, but cleaning and sanitization will be significantly reduced.

title: Wash Motor Over-Current
shortDescription: The wash motor is drawing excessive current, triggering over-current protection.
fullDescription: Fault F8 indicates the wash pump motor is drawing more current than the safe limit. This protects the motor and control board from damage. Common causes include a seized wash impeller, worn motor bearings, or a shorted motor winding.

causes.cause: Foreign object jamming the wash impeller
Faulty motor control circuit on main board

symptoms: Wash motor hums but does not spin
Machine stops immediately after starting wash phase

fixSteps.title: Check for impeller obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Look into the sump for broken glass, food debris, or other objects that may be jamming the wash impeller.

fixSteps.title: Clear the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: If debris is visible, carefully remove it. Try turning the impeller by hand — it should rotate freely. If it is stuck, the motor may need removal.

fixSteps.title: Test motor windings
fixSteps.instruction: Access the wash motor and disconnect it. Test each winding with a multimeter. Check for shorts between windings and from windings to ground.

fixSteps.title: Replace wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is seized or shorted, replace the entire wash motor assembly. Disconnect all hoses and wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new motor.

whenToCallPro: If the impeller is clear but the motor is still drawing excessive current, professional motor replacement is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F8 wash motor overcurrent fix

faq.q: What does F8 mean?
faq.a: F8 means the wash motor is drawing too much current. Usually caused by a jammed impeller or worn motor.

faq.q: Can broken glass cause F8?
faq.a: Yes. Broken glass fragments can jam the wash impeller, causing the motor to stall and draw excessive current.

faq.q: How can I prevent F8?
faq.a: Rinse dishes before loading to prevent food debris from reaching the impeller. Check for and remove broken glass immediately.

title: Overflow Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The overflow sensor has detected water above the maximum safe level.
fullDescription: Fault F9 occurs when water in the dishwasher tub rises above the maximum safe level detected by the overflow sensor. This triggers an emergency drain to prevent flooding. Causes include a stuck-open inlet valve, failed water level sensor, or a drain blockage combined with continued filling.

causes.cause: Inlet valve stuck open allowing continuous filling
Faulty water level or pressure sensor
Drain blockage preventing water removal during fill
Control board not sending valve shutoff signal

symptoms: Water visible above normal level inside tub
Water leaking from door or base
Machine shuts down and will not restart

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply tap to the dishwasher. Unplug the machine. Place towels to contain any water.

fixSteps.title: Drain excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Use a cup or sponge to remove excess water from the tub. Check the drain filter for blockages that may have prevented normal draining.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With supply off and dishwasher unplugged, test the inlet valve solenoid. Also check if the valve drips when powered off — a stuck valve will continue letting water through.

fixSteps.title: Check water level sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the pressure sensor or float switch. Test for proper operation and correct readings. Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the valve and sensor both test fine, the control board may have a relay fault requiring professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher F9 overflow sensor fix

faq.q: What does F9 mean?
faq.a: F9 means water has risen above the safe maximum level. Turn off the water supply immediately.

faq.q: Can F9 cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes, if the inlet valve is stuck open and the overflow is not contained. Turn off the water supply as soon as F9 appears.

faq.q: How do I prevent F9?
faq.a: Regularly clean the drain filter and have the inlet valve inspected if you notice slow draining.

title: Diverter Valve Fault
shortDescription: The wash diverter valve is not switching between spray arms correctly.
fullDescription: Fault FA indicates the diverter valve, which directs water between the upper and lower spray arms, is not operating properly. This can result in only one spray arm receiving water, leading to poor wash results on one rack.
symptoms: Top rack dishes not clean (only bottom arm works)
Bottom rack dishes not clean (only top arm works)

fixSteps.title: Clean spray arms and diverter area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove both spray arms and clear any debris from the spray arm ports and the diverter valve area. Food particles can jam the diverter mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Check diverter operation
fixSteps.instruction: During a wash cycle, listen for the diverter switching sound. If you hear the motor but no switching occurs, the mechanism may be jammed or the valve is broken.

fixSteps.title: Test diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the diverter motor (usually under the tub). Disconnect and test with a multimeter for continuity. Also test by applying the rated voltage to confirm the motor turns.

fixSteps.title: Replace diverter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor or valve mechanism is faulty, replace the entire diverter assembly. Disconnect hoses and wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new unit.

whenToCallPro: If the diverter motor and mechanism test fine, the control board output may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher FA diverter valve fix

faq.q: What does FA mean?
faq.a: FA means the diverter valve that switches water between spray arms is not working properly.

faq.q: Why is only one rack getting clean?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between racks. If it is stuck, only one spray arm receives water.

faq.q: Is the diverter valve hard to replace?
faq.a: It is moderately difficult. Access is underneath the dishwasher tub and requires removing hoses and wiring.

title: Rinse Aid Empty
shortDescription: The rinse aid dispenser is empty or the level sensor has detected a low level.
fullDescription: Alarm A1 is a service reminder indicating the rinse aid dispenser needs refilling. This is not a critical fault and the dishwasher will continue to operate, but drying performance and spot-free results will be reduced without rinse aid.

causes.cause: Rinse aid dispenser lid not sealing properly

symptoms: A1 indicator or alert on display
Water spots on glasses and cutlery

fixSteps.title: Refill the rinse aid
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rinse aid dispenser cap on the inside of the door. Fill with rinse aid until the level reaches the full mark. Close the cap securely.

fixSteps.title: Check the dispenser seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rinse aid cap and its gasket. Ensure the cap closes tightly. Replace the cap if the gasket is worn or missing.

fixSteps.title: Adjust rinse aid dosage
fixSteps.instruction: If rinse aid empties too quickly, reduce the dosage setting. Most Fisher & Paykel dishwashers have a dial near the dispenser to adjust the amount dispensed per cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: If the rinse aid empties very quickly despite low dosage, the dispenser may be cracked. Inspect the reservoir for cracks or leaks.

whenToCallPro: This rarely requires professional service. Only if the dispenser reservoir itself is cracked.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher A1 rinse aid refill

faq.q: What does A1 mean?
faq.a: A1 is a reminder that the rinse aid dispenser needs refilling. It is not a machine fault.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without rinse aid?
faq.a: Yes, but you may get water spots on glasses and dishes may not dry as well.

faq.q: How often do I need to refill rinse aid?
faq.a: Typically every 1-3 months depending on usage frequency and dosage setting.

title: Salt Level Low
shortDescription: The water softener salt level is low and needs to be replenished.
fullDescription: Alarm A2 indicates the salt reservoir for the built-in water softener is running low. The water softener reduces mineral content in hard water areas to prevent limescale buildup on dishes and internal components. Without salt, the softener cannot regenerate.

causes.cause: Salt reservoir depleted through normal use
Salt not dissolving properly (wrong type used)

symptoms: White limescale spots on dishes and glasses
Reduced cleaning performance in hard water areas

fixSteps.title: Refill the salt reservoir
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the salt reservoir cap at the bottom of the dishwasher tub. Unscrew the cap. Fill with dishwasher salt (not table salt) until full. Replace the cap securely.

fixSteps.title: Clean up spilled salt
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe up any salt that spilled into the tub. Run a short rinse cycle to flush salt residue, which can cause corrosion if left on stainless steel.

fixSteps.title: Check water hardness setting
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water hardness setting matches your local water supply. Incorrect settings can cause the softener to use salt too quickly or not enough.

fixSteps.title: Inspect salt reservoir seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the cap seal for wear. If the seal is damaged, salt water can leak and corrode internal components. Replace the cap if needed.

whenToCallPro: This is a routine maintenance task that rarely requires professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher A2 salt refill

faq.q: What does A2 mean?
faq.a: A2 means the water softener salt level is low. Refill with dishwasher salt.

faq.q: Can I use table salt?
faq.a: No. Use only dishwasher-specific salt. Table salt contains additives that can damage the water softener.

faq.q: Do I need salt if I have soft water?
faq.a: In areas with very soft water, you may not need the water softener. Check your local water hardness level.

title: Filter Clean Reminder
shortDescription: The dishwasher is due for a filter cleaning based on the usage counter.
fullDescription: Alarm A3 is a maintenance reminder indicating the drain filter should be cleaned. The machine tracks cycle count and activates this reminder periodically. A dirty filter reduces wash performance and can lead to drain faults.

causes.cause: Filter cleaning overdue based on cycle count
Filter assembly damaged or not seated properly

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom dish rack. Twist and lift out the cylindrical filter assembly. Rinse all parts under running water, using a soft brush to remove trapped food debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump area
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, check the sump area for any debris, glass fragments, or food buildup. Wipe clean with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Place the filter assembly back into the sump and twist to lock it into position. Ensure it sits flush and secure.

fixSteps.title: Reset the reminder
fixSteps.instruction: The A3 alarm typically clears automatically after a cycle. If it does not, consult the manual for the reset procedure (usually pressing and holding a specific button combination).

whenToCallPro: This is basic maintenance that does not require professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher A3 filter cleaning

faq.q: What does A3 mean?
faq.a: A3 is a reminder to clean the drain filter. It is not a fault — just routine maintenance.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Ideally after every few cycles, or at least once a week for heavy use.

faq.q: Will A3 clear on its own?
faq.a: It typically clears after cleaning the filter and running a cycle. A manual reset may be needed on some models.

title: Door Not Latched Properly
shortDescription: The dishwasher door is not fully closed or the latch is not engaged.
fullDescription: Alarm A4 indicates the door latch switch is not detecting a secure closure. The dishwasher cannot start a cycle unless the door is fully latched for safety reasons. The latch mechanism, door hinge, or latch switch may need attention.

causes.cause: Dish rack or utensil protruding and preventing closure
Worn or damaged door latch mechanism

symptoms: Door appears closed but machine does not respond
Latch clicks but does not hold

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and ensure no dishes, utensils, or racks are protruding beyond the door line. Adjust items and close the door firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the latch mechanism on the door top and the strike plate on the tub frame. Check for damage, warping, or debris preventing engagement.

fixSteps.title: Adjust door hinges
fixSteps.instruction: If the door has sagged, the latch may not align with the strike. Adjust the door hinges or tighten hinge screws to restore proper alignment.

fixSteps.title: Replace door latch
fixSteps.instruction: If the latch mechanism is worn or broken, replace it. Remove the inner door panel to access the latch. Disconnect and replace with a new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the latch and switch both appear fine but the alarm persists, the wiring or control board input may need professional attention.
videoSearchQuery: Fisher Paykel dishwasher A4 door latch fix

faq.q: What does A4 mean?
faq.a: A4 means the door is not fully latched. Check for obstructions and close the door firmly.

faq.q: Why won't the dishwasher start?
faq.a: For safety, the dishwasher requires the door to be securely latched before any cycle can begin.

faq.q: Can a worn latch cause A4?
faq.a: Yes. Over time, the latch mechanism can wear, preventing proper engagement. Replacement is straightforward.
